answers.problem: SMB SaaS companies struggle to prioritize which external vulnerabilities deserve immediate attention.
answers.icp: B2B SaaS companies with 20 to 200 employees, one security lead or fractional CISO, and limited AppSec maturity.
answers.current_solution: They use scanners, spreadsheets, ticket backlogs, and occasional consulting projects.
answers.why_now: Existing tools flood teams with findings but do not translate them into business-priority remediation decisions.
answers.advantage: Deep offensive-security experience plus founder access to likely early design partners.
answers.mvp: A dashboard that ingests external findings, scores exploitability and business exposure, and outputs a prioritized remediation queue.
answers.payment_reason: It reduces breach exposure and saves senior security time by focusing remediation on what matters most.
answers.first_10_customers: Warm outbound to founder network, LinkedIn content, and direct outreach to Brazilian B2B SaaS security leaders.
answers.early_success: 10 discovery calls, 3 pilots, 2 paying customers, and weekly product usage by pilot teams in 60 days.
answers.killer_risks: Weak willingness to pay, long security-sales cycles, and inability to show meaningful prioritization accuracy over existing tools.
